title: RNA Isolation from Plant Tissue Protocol 15: Hot Acid Phenol Method for Algae

final reaction buffer was filter purified using Nalgene 0.22 μM filter.","type":"unstyled"}],"entityMap":{},"guid":"65E6C7039BFD44D2B5661D690EB2C518"}}}}
steps: Preheat phenol and phenol:chloroform to 65 °C . Heated phenol should not be re-used.
Collect algae cells via centrifugation for 00:10:00 at at Room temperature .
Re-suspend frozen pellet in 800 µL of preheated extraction buffer .
Immediately add 800 µL of hot acid phenol.
Incubate at 65 °C for 00:10:00 . Vortex every 1 min for 00:00:10 .
Centrifuge at at 4 °C for 00:05:00 .
The aqueous phase was transferred to fresh 1.5 ml micro-centrifuge tube.
Repeat steps 5 - 7. Repeat 3x (depending on the amount of cells used). go to step #5
Extract with equal volume of phenol:chloroform (5:1).
Transfer aqueous phase to a new 1.5 ml microfuge tube. Volume should be ~ 700 µL .
Add 1/10 volume of 3 Molarity (M) sodium acetate, pH 5.5, and 1 volume of isopropanol.
Spin in micro-centrifuge at 4 °C at top speed for 00:20:00 .
Remove the supernatant without dislodging the pellet.
Wash the pellet with 70 % ethanol.
Invert and air dry tubes at room temperature.
Flash freeze pellets with liquid nitrogen and keep at -80 °C until extractions are carried out.
Vortex the tubes for 00:00:15 .
Vortex for 00:01:00 at Room temperature .
Spin for 00:05:00 in a microcentrifuge at top speed.
Repeat step 9 three times. go to step #9
Hold at 4 °C for 00:30:00 or more.
The pellet was re-suspended in 50 µL DEPC treated H 2 O.
The RNAs were stored at -20 °C .
creator: Eric Carpenter
